LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE TWO PARAGRAPHS THAT RELATE TO WRONGFUL DEATH BECAUSE "CALIFORNIA CASES HAVE UNIFORMLY HELD THAT DAMAGES FOR MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, INCLUDING GRIEF AND SORROW, ARE NOT RECOVERABLE IN A WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION." KROUSE V. GRAHAM, (1977) 19 CAL.3RD 59, 72. AS TO THE MEDICAL MALPRACTICE PARAGRAPHS, THE MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO RECOVER FOR THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTIONS. HOWEVER, AS STATED ABOVE, PLAINTIFF CANNOT RECOVER FROM THIS DEFENDANT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF AND THIS DEFENDANT DID NOT HAVE A PHYSICIAN-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THESE PARAGRAPHS. BECAUSE THE ESTATE DOES NOT APPEAR TO HAVE A CAPACITY TO BRING THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, IT FOLLOWS THAT IT WOULD BE PROPER TO STRIKE ITS PRAYER FOR DAMAGES. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE PRAYERS FOR RELIEF IN THE MEDICAL MALPRACTICE AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE THEY ARE DUPLICATIVE AND PROPERLY ASSERTED IN THE WRONGFUL DEATH CAUSE OF ACTION. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IT HAS LONG BEEN HELD IN CALIFORNIA LAW THAT AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT TO A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL MALPRACTICE IS A PHYSICIAN-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP GIVING RISE TO A DUTY OF CARE. MERO V. SADOFF, (1995) 31 CA 4TH 1466, 1471. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT NEVER HAD A PHYSICIAN-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP AND THUS PLAINTIFF MAY MOT BRING THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT 20 DAYS FROM TODAY (04/04/11) AS AGREED TO BY COUNSEL. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BY PRESENTING EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT FELL WITHIN THE APPLICABLE STANDARD OF CARE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. WHETHER SURGERY SHOULD HAVE BEEN PERFORMED IMMEDIATELY, WHETHER DR. LEICHTLING'S DECISION TO FIRST TRY TO STABILIZE THE PATIENT WAS NEGLIGENT, AND WHETHER IMMEDIATE SURGERY WOULD HAVE CHANGED THE OUTCOME ARE NOT ISSUES WITHIN COMMON KNOWLEDGE. DEFENDANT'S EXPERT TESTIFIES THAT SURGERY COULD NOT BE PERFORMED IMMEDIATELY AND IF SURGERY HAD BEEN ATTEMPTED WITHOUT STEPS BEING TAKEN TO STABILIZE PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE DIED ON THE OPERATING TABLE. (SCHULTZ DECL. EX. B, GARDINER DECL. AT 8, 10.) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SUBMIT ANY EVIDENCE, LET ALONE ANY EXPERT EVIDENCE, THAT WOULD CONTRADICT THIS EXPERT OPINION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEPT. 504: MOTION TO STRIKE AND TAX COSTS AND OBJECTIONS GRANTED. COURT ADOPTS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS' C.C.P. SECTION 998 OFFERS OF DISMISSAL WITH WAIVER OF COSTS WERE TOKEN OFFERS WITH NO REASONABLE PROSPECT OF ACCEPTANCE. BASED ON THE COURT'S UNDERDERSTANDING OF THE EVIDENCE, HAVING HEARD THE TRIAL TESTIMONY, THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT, HOWEVER STRONG THEIR POSITIONS MIGHT HAVE BEEN, DEFENDANTS COULD NOT REASONABLY BELIEVE THEY FACED NO POTENTIAL FOR LIABILITY, AND, HAD THE JURY FOUND LIABILITY, DAMAGES WOULD LIKELY HAVE BEEN SUBSTANTIAL. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED OVERCOMES THE PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE OF REASONABLENESS ARISING FROM THE DEFENSE VERDICT. THE COURT ALSO DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO AWARD COSTS FOR DEFENDANTS' INVESTIGATIVE SUBPOENAS. ANY PARTY WISHING TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING MUST SO ADVISE THE COURTROOM CLERK (415-551-3772) AND OPPOSING COUNSEL BY 4:30 P.M THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 12, 2013.

ALTHOUGH OFFERED A HEARING DATE OF AUGUST 9, 2013, WITHIN THE 60-DAY STATUTORY PERIOD, PLAINTIFFS CHOSE A HEARING DATE OF SEPTEMBER 13, AFTER THE TIME HAD EXPIRED. PLAINTIFFS "AMENDED" AND SUPPLEMENTAL PLEADINGS WERE FILED AFTER THE 60-DAY PERIOD HAD ALREADY RUN. IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT WOULD HAVE DENIED THE MOTION ON ITS MERITS. THE ISSUES CONCERNING VOIR DIRE AND THE JUROR NOTEBOOK WERE ALREADY HEARD AND DECIDED ON THE RECORD DURING TRIAL. THE COURT SEES NO BASIS TO ALTER ITS RULING. THE DECLARATION OF THE ONE DISSATISFIED JUROR IS INSUFFICIENT TO CONVINCE THE COURT OF JURY MISCONDUCT SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT A NEW TRIAL. ANY PARTY WISHING TO CONTEST THIS TENTATIVE RULING BY APPEARING AT THE HEARING MUST SO ADVISE THE COURTROOM CLERK (415-551-3772) AND OPPOSING COUNSEL BY 4:30 P.M. THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 12, 2013. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: AARON SCHULTZ, ESQ., 925.930.9090, FOR DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by presenting evidence showing that Defendant's conduct fell within the applicable standard of care. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Whether surgery should have been performed immediately, whether Dr. Leichtling's decision to first try to stabilize the patient was negligent, and whether immediate surgery would have changed the outcome are not issues within common knowledge. Defendant's expert testifies that surgery could not be performed immediately and if surgery had been attempted without steps being taken to stabilize Plaintiff, Plaintiff would have died on the operating table. (Schultz Decl. Ex. B, Gardiner Decl. at 8, 10.) Plaintiff fails to submit any evidence, let alone any expert evidence, that would contradict this expert opinion. Order signed in open Court. JUDGMENT signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Matt Springman, Esq. of the Law Office of Waukeen McCoy, Tel. 703.217.7018, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party;

The matter was discussed. The court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Ivan Burgos M.D., Heriberto Ocasio M.D.'S (Joinder) Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Simply filing a notice of joinder is not sufficient for purposes of a motion for summary judgment and each party must file their own separate statement. (Frazee v. Seely, (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627, 636.) Furthermore, the joinder fails to comply with CCP 437c(a). (See Lerma v. County of Orange, (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 709, 718.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Shyamala T. Rajender, Esq. of Law Offices of Rajender Law Offices 675-7705 for pltfs & opposing parties; Melanie M. Witte, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard 334-6880 for Regents defts; Karen A. Sparks, Esq. of Galloway Lucchese Everson & Picchi 925/930-9090 for deft Jonathan Leichtling, M.D.; Sonja M. Dahl, Esq. of Donnelly Nelson Depolo & Muray 925/287-8181 for deft Lauren Friedly, M.D.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified:
The Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. It has long been held in California law that an essential element to a cause of action for medical malpractice is a physician-patient relationship giving rise to a duty of care. Mero v. Sadoff, (1995) 31 CA 4th 1466, 1471. Plaintiff and defendant never had a physician-patient relationship and thus plaintiff may mot bring this cause of action against this defendant.
The Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.
The Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.
The demurrer to the 6th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend.
The demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.
Plaintiff to file amended complaint 20 days from today (04/04/11) as agreed to by counsel. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Shyamala T. Rajender, Esq. of Law Offices of Rajender Law Offices 675-7705 for pltfs & opposing parties; Melanie M. Witte, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard 334-6880 for Regents defts; Karen A. Sparks, Esq. of Galloway Lucchese Everson & Picchi 925/930-9090 for deft Jonathan Leichtling, M.D.; Sonja M. Dahl, Esq. of Donnelly Nelson Depolo & Muray 925/287-8181 for deft Lauren Friedly, M.D.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion to Strike is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to the two paragraphs that relate to wrongful death because "California cases have uniformly held that damages for mental and emotional distress, including grief and sorrow, are not recoverable in a wrongful death action." Krouse v. Graham, (1977) 19 Cal.3rd 59, 72. As to the medical malpractice paragraphs, the motion is denied. Plaintiff may be able to recover for the negligence cause of actions. HOWEVER, as stated above, plaintiff cannot recover from this defendant because plaintiff and this defendant did not have a physician-patient relationship.
The Motion is granted as to these paragraphs. Because the Estate does not appear to have a capacity to bring this cause of action, it follows that it would be proper to strike its prayer for damages.
The Motion is granted as to the prayers for relief in the Medical Malpractice and Emotional Distress causes of action because they are duplicative and properly asserted in the Wrongful Death cause of action. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
